title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Go Client Systems · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A platform SDK team integrating a staging control plane must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for https, tls identity, certificate trust, proxies, and downgrade boundaries, producing transport trust and certificate evidence record. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Go 1.26 net/http transport trust and certificate evidence record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Preserve hostname, chain, validity, algorithm, and revocation-policy checks without disabling verification. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Add an intended private trust root or test certificate without replacing or weakening unrelated trust. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Https Identity
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Go staging control-plane client connects to api3.internal.test through proxy.example.test using a private CA and optional client certificate. Verify requested identity, connected endpoint, roots, proxy authority, downgrade policy, and what evidence the browser lab cannot supply. Competency probe: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Https Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bind https authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Https Identity, keep Https Identity connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
